w:r.w:t: Built on MakerKit Next.js SaaS Starter Kit Lite
Pages · Modals · Navigation · Dashboard · Database · Real-Time · Tech Stack
MakerKit Next.js SaaS Starter Kit Lite (GitHub)
Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui — Free & Open Source
Technology choices, MakerKit setup, free services
Every page with full UI element breakdown
Metrics, widgets, charts, and data shown
How users move through the entire app
100% Free — Open Source & Free Tier Services
1.1 Why MakerKit Next.js SaaS Starter Kit Lite?

MakerKit's Lite starter gives you a production-ready Next.js 14 app with authentication, team management, billing stubs, and basic UI wired up. Instead of spending weeks on auth and setup, you skip straight to building your order management features. Here's exactly what it provides out of the box:
Basic dashboard layout with sidebar navigation
Row Level Security (RLS) helpers for Supabase
Full-stack React framework. App Router for pages, layouts, API routes. Server components for fast loads.
Type safety across all components and API routes. Catches bugs at compile time.
User registration, login, session management. Built into MakerKit. Handles JWT tokens.
PostgreSQL database for all orders, customers, users, notifications, timeline events.
WebSocket subscriptions for live order updates and real-time browser notifications.
Utility-first CSS framework for all styling. Zero design files needed.
Pre-built accessible React components: modals, dropdowns, tables, badges, etc.
Lightweight global state for notification bell count, user session, toast queue.
Server state management, caching, background refetching for order lists and stats.
Form state and validation for Create Order, Edit Customer forms.
Schema validation for all form inputs and API request bodies.
Date formatting for timeline entries, notification timestamps.
Icon library used throughout the UI (status icons, nav icons, action buttons).
Dashboard charts: order volume bar chart, status donut chart.
Deployment platform. Serverless functions, edge CDN, preview deploys.
Source control and CI/CD trigger for Vercel auto-deploys.
Fork or clone https://github.com/makerkit/nextjs-saas-starter-kit-lite to your GitHub
Create a free Supabase project at supabase.com — get your Project URL and anon key
Copy .env.example to .env.local and fill in your Supabase credentials
Run npm install and npm run dev — the app boots with auth, dashboard shell, and sidebar
Connect your GitHub repo to Vercel for automatic deployments on every push
Run the SQL migrations from Section 10 of this document in Supabase SQL Editor
Start building your order management pages on top of the existing layout
Folder structure, routing, and data flow
MakerKit uses Next.js 14 App Router. Every folder inside /app is a route. Layouts wrap child pages. Here is the complete folder structure for this project:
Shared layout: sidebar + topbar + notification provider
Dashboard / Home page with KPI widgets and charts
All Orders list page with filters and search
Single Order detail page with timeline
Create New Order page with customer autocomplete
GET all orders, POST create order
GET single order, PATCH update, DELETE cancel
PATCH — update order status + create timeline event
GET all notifications, PATCH mark as read
TypeScript interfaces: Order, Customer, Notification, TimelineEvent
Every page, every element, every interaction
These pages come pre-built from MakerKit. Minimal customization needed — just add your logo and brand colors.
Email input field, Password input field, 'Sign In' primary button, 'Forgot Password?' link, App logo top center, Error alert component (wrong credentials), Loading spinner on submit
On success → redirect to /dashboard. On fail → show inline error. Session stored in Supabase cookie.
Full Name input, Email input, Password input, Confirm Password input, 'Create Account' button, Link to Login page
Creates Supabase auth user + inserts into users table with 'active' status. Redirects to dashboard.
The main landing page after login. Gives an instant snapshot of the entire operation. Split into clear zones from top to bottom:
'+ New Order' primary CTA button — top right corner — opens Create Order modal
Notification bell icon with unread count badge (red dot or number)
User avatar dropdown: Profile, Settings, Sign Out
Zone B — KPI Cards Row (4 cards across)
Four stat cards in a horizontal row. Each card contains an icon, label, number value, and a small trend indicator (up/down arrow with % change vs yesterday):
Card 1: Total Orders Today — e.g. '24' — with package icon
Card 2: Pending Orders — e.g. '8' — with clock icon — clickable, filters order list
Card 3: Out for Delivery — e.g. '5' — with truck icon
Card 4: Delivered Today — e.g. '11' — with checkmark icon
Zone C — Charts Row (2 charts side by side)
Left Chart (60% width): 'Order Volume — Last 7 Days' — vertical bar chart using Recharts. X-axis = day name, Y-axis = count. Bars color-coded by status breakdown (stacked).
Right Chart (40% width): 'Current Status Distribution' — donut / pie chart. Each slice = one status. Legend below with count and percentage. Uses shadcn/ui Card wrapper.
Section title: 'Recent Orders' with 'View All' link on right
Table with columns: Order ID, Customer Name, Mobile, Status Badge, Created By, Created At, Actions
Showing last 10 orders only on dashboard
Status column shows colored badge (green for Delivered, orange for Out for Delivery, etc.)
Actions column: 'View' button links to order detail page
Clicking any row navigates to order detail page
Zone E — Activity Feed (Right sidebar panel on wide screens)
Scrollable list of the last 15 timeline events across all orders
Each item: Status icon, 'Order #ORD-001 marked as Delivered', timestamp, user avatar
The primary working page. Users spend most of their time here. Comprehensive list with powerful filtering:
Left side: Search input — placeholder 'Search by Order ID, customer name, or mobile...'
Status filter dropdown — options: All Statuses, Pending, Accepted, Ready for Delivery, Out for Delivery, Delivered, Cancelled
'Created By' dropdown — filter by specific user
Right side: '+ New Order' button (same as dashboard)
Sort control: 'Sort by: Newest First' dropdown with options
Columns: Checkbox (bulk select), Order ID, Customer Name, Mobile Number, Status, Created By, Created At, Last Updated, Actions
Order ID: Clickable link — '#ORD-0047' format — opens Order Detail page
Status column: Colored badge component — each status has unique color
Actions column per row: 'View' icon button, Quick Status Update dropdown (chevron icon), 'Cancel' icon button (red, with confirmation)
Bulk actions bar (appears when checkboxes selected): 'Cancel Selected', 'Export Selected'
Pagination: 'Previous / Next' buttons + page number input + 'per page' selector (25, 50, 100)
Empty state: Illustration + 'No orders found. Try adjusting your filters or create a new order.' + 'Create Order' button
Loading state: Skeleton rows with shimmer animation
The most information-dense page. Split into two columns on desktop, single column on mobile:
Page header: 'Order #ORD-0047' with back arrow to order list
Status badge (large) + 'Cancel Order' button (red, right side of header)
Order Info Card: Order ID, Created By (name + avatar), Created At (full datetime), Last Updated
Customer Details Card: Customer Name, Mobile Number (with click-to-call link), any additional fields
Current Status Card: Big colored status pill, description of what this status means, action buttons below
Status Action Buttons: Context-aware buttons based on current status — 'Mark as Accepted', 'Mark Ready for Delivery', etc. Also a 'Move Back' secondary button to revert one step
Notes / remarks section if any notes were added during status update
Vertical timeline component — newest event at top
Each timeline entry: Colored dot matching status color, Status name in bold, 'by [User Name]' in smaller text, Full date and time (e.g. '5 Mar 2026, 2:34 PM'), Optional note/remark in italic
First entry always: 'Order Created — Pending' with creation details
Smooth scroll if timeline is long
'Add Note' option (optional): Small text area to add a remark without changing status
A dedicated full-page form (not a modal) for creating orders. Clean two-column layout on desktop:
Section header: 'Customer Details' with customer icon
Mobile Number field (FIRST field — most important): Large input, triggers autocomplete
Autocomplete Dropdown (appears while typing): Search result cards showing 'Name — Mobile — Last Order Date'. Up to 5 suggestions. Keyboard navigable. Click selects and auto-fills remaining fields.
Customer Name field: Auto-filled if existing customer selected, otherwise manual entry
Additional fields (optional, extensible): Address, Email, any other customer data you add
'New Customer' indicator: Small badge appears if no match found — 'This will create a new customer record'
Order ID: Auto-generated, displayed read-only — '#ORD-XXXX' format
Created By: Auto-populated from logged-in user's name — read-only
Status: Shows 'Pending / Order Created' — read-only on creation
Notes / Remarks: Optional textarea for any initial notes
'Create Order' primary button — validates form, submits, shows success toast, redirects to new order detail page
'Cancel' secondary button — navigates back to order list with unsaved changes warning dialog
Inline validation errors below each field
Full-page loading overlay on submit to prevent double submission
A directory of all unique customers tracked by mobile number. Useful for looking up customer history:
Search bar: 'Search by name or mobile number'
Customer cards in a grid (or table rows): Name, Mobile, Total Orders count, Last Order date, Last Order status badge
Click customer card → navigates to a customer detail view showing all their orders
No create button here — customers are created through orders only
Dedicated page to view all past notifications. Not just a panel — a full searchable archive:
Page title: 'Notifications' with 'Mark All as Read' button
Search bar: 'Search notifications...' — searches notification message text
Date filter: Today, This Week, Custom Range
Notification list: Each item shows icon (colored by type), Message text, Order ID link, Time ('2 hours ago' or full date), Read/Unread indicator (bold text = unread)
Clicking notification → navigates to the related order detail page and marks notification as read
Infinite scroll or pagination for older notifications
Empty state: 'You're all caught up!' with bell illustration
Profile tab: Update full name, profile photo upload
Account tab: Change email, change password
Notifications tab: Toggle preferences — 'Receive browser notifications for new orders', 'Receive browser notifications for status updates'
No team/billing features needed since this is internal only
Triggered from: Order table row 'Quick Update' button, Order detail page action buttons. This is the most frequently used modal in the entire app.
Current status shown at top with colored badge
Status selection: Visual stepper / pill selector showing all 5 stages. Currently active stage highlighted. User clicks desired stage.
Forward navigation: Highlighted green — normal progression
Backward navigation: Highlighted amber with warning icon — 'Moving status backward'
Cancel Order: Red outlined button below the stepper — separate destructive action
Notes textarea: 'Add a note about this status change (optional)'
Footer: 'Update Status' primary button (disabled until new status selected), 'Cancel' secondary button
On success: Modal closes, toast notification 'Order #ORD-0047 updated to Out for Delivery', order list/detail refreshes automatically
Triggered from: Red 'Cancel Order' button on order detail page or bulk cancel in order list.
Body text: 'This will cancel Order #ORD-0047 for [Customer Name]. This action cannot be undone but the order record will remain in the system.'
Reason for cancellation textarea (optional but encouraged)
Footer: 'Yes, Cancel Order' (red destructive button), 'Keep Order' (secondary button)
On confirm: Order status set to 'Cancelled', timeline entry created, notification sent to all users
Triggered from: Bell icon in topbar. Slides in from the right side — does not cover full screen.
Panel title: 'Notifications' with 'Mark all read' text button
Unread count badge in panel header
Each item: Type icon, Message, Time ago, Read/unread dot indicator, Clickable — navigates to order and closes panel
'View All Notifications' link at bottom — navigates to full notifications page
Close button (X) top right, or click outside panel to close
Triggered from: User avatar in topbar (top-right of layout). Small dropdown menu:
User name and email at top (read-only header)
Menu items: Profile & Settings (links to /settings), My Orders (filters order list to current user), Notification Preferences
Triggered when: User navigates away from Create Order form with data entered.
Body: 'You have unsaved changes. If you leave now, your order draft will be lost.'
Appears as a dropdown directly in the order table row — not a full modal. For power users who want quick updates without opening a modal:
Triggered by clicking the chevron icon next to status badge in order table
Shows dropdown list of all valid next statuses (forward only from this shortcut)
Click a status → immediately updates with a confirmation toast. No extra modal.
Does NOT include backward movement or cancel — those require the full modal
Not a modal — an inline dropdown that appears below the Mobile Number input on the Create Order page:
Result items: Customer name (bold), Mobile number, 'Last order: 2 days ago' in small gray text
'No matching customer — a new record will be created' if no results
Keyboard support: Arrow keys to navigate, Enter to select, Escape to dismiss
On selection: Closes dropdown, auto-fills Name and any other stored customer fields
How users move through the entire application
All authenticated pages share the same layout wrapper from MakerKit's (dashboard) layout:
Left Sidebar (fixed width, always visible on desktop, collapsible on tablet, drawer on mobile)
Top Navigation Bar (full width, fixed at top)
Main Content Area (scrollable, takes remaining space)
Vertical navigation menu. Active item highlighted with brand color background:
Plus-circle icon — /orders/new — Create order form (highlighted differently as a CTA)
Bell icon with badge — /notifications — Full notification history
User avatar, name, and sign out button pinned to sidebar bottom
Left: Hamburger menu icon (mobile only) to open sidebar drawer
Right: Quick search icon (opens global search overlay)
Right: Notification bell with unread count badge
Right: User avatar with dropdown menu trigger
Flow A: Creating a New Order
User lands on Dashboard or Orders page
Clicks '+ New Order' button (topbar or sidebar)
Types mobile number → autocomplete suggestions appear → selects existing customer OR types new customer name
Clicks 'Create Order' → validation runs → order created
All other logged-in users receive a real-time browser notification
Finds order by search or scroll
Clicks 'View' button OR clicks Order ID link → goes to /orders/[id]
Sees current status and action buttons
Clicks appropriate status action button (e.g. 'Mark as Accepted')
Status Update Modal appears with status stepper
User optionally adds a note → clicks 'Update Status'
Modal closes, page updates in real-time, timeline entry added
Other users get a notification: 'Order #ORD-0051 is now Accepted'
User clicks bell → Notification Panel slides in from right
Clicks one notification → panel closes, navigates to relevant order page
Alternatively clicks 'View All Notifications' → goes to /notifications
On /notifications: can search, filter, and view full history
Sees customer grid — searches for a mobile number
Clicks customer card → customer detail view
Sees all orders for this customer with timeline of each
Default start state. Order exists, no action taken yet.
Team has acknowledged and accepted the order.
Order is packed and ready to be picked up by delivery.
Delivery is en route to the customer.
Customer has received the order. Final successful state.
Order was cancelled. Can happen from any stage.
The system allows both forward and backward movement, plus cancellation from any stage:
Forward: Any status can advance to the next sequential status
Backward: Any status can revert to the previous status (with a warning in UI)
Cancel: Available from any status except 'Delivered' — shows confirmation dialog
Delivered orders: Cannot be cancelled or moved backward — they are the terminal success state
Cancelled orders: Status is locked — no further transitions possible
Every transition — forward, backward, or cancel — creates a new timeline entry in the order_timeline table. No transitions are silent.
Each timeline event records exactly who did what and when:
order_id: Reference to the parent order
from_status: What the status was before (null for initial creation)
to_status: What the status changed to
changed_by_name: Denormalized user name for display performance
note: Optional text remark added during status change
In-app, real-time, and browser push notifications
There are two layers of notifications working together:
Stored in the 'notifications' database table. Persisted. Visible in notification panel and /notifications page. Supports read/unread state. Searchable history.
WebSocket events via Supabase Realtime. Ephemeral — not stored. Shows as a toast popup in the browser corner. Also triggers the bell badge count increase.
Supabase Realtime listens to database changes via WebSockets. Here's the implementation approach:
Subscribe to the 'orders' table for INSERT and UPDATE events
Subscribe to the 'notifications' table for INSERT events
On INSERT in orders → broadcast to all connected clients: show toast 'New Order Created'
On UPDATE in orders (status change) → broadcast: show toast with new status
On INSERT in notifications → update bell badge count in Zustand store
The Supabase Realtime channel is initialized in a React Context Provider that wraps the entire dashboard layout — so all pages share one connection
useEffect in the Realtime provider subscribes on mount, unsubscribes on unmount to prevent memory leaks
Real-time toasts appear in the bottom-right corner of the screen using shadcn/ui Sonner or Toaster:
New Order: Blue background, package icon, 'New Order Created — #ORD-0051 for [Name]', 'View Order' action link
Status Update: Color matches new status color, appropriate icon, order link
Cancelled: Red toast with warning icon
Maximum 3 toasts visible simultaneously — older ones queue below
User can manually dismiss by clicking X on each toast
TEXT NOT NULL — Display name used in order attribution and timeline
BOOLEAN DEFAULT true — Soft disable users without deleting
TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE — Primary lookup key, indexed
TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE — e.g. 'ORD-0047' — auto-generated via trigger
JSONB — Snapshot of customer name, mobile at time of order creation
TEXT NOT NULL — Denormalized for display
TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending' — CHECK constraint: one of the 6 valid statuses
BOOLEAN DEFAULT false — Quick filter helper
UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
TEXT — NULL for initial creation entry
TEXT — Optional remark about this status change
TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now() — The exact time of the change
UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) — Who this notification is for
UUID REFERENCES orders(id) — Related order (nullable for system notifs)
TEXT NOT NULL — Human-readable notification text
orders(created_at DESC) — for sorting by newest
notifications(user_id, is_read) — for unread count queries
order_timeline(order_id, created_at DESC) — for timeline display
Since this is an internal tool where all authenticated users can see all orders, the RLS policy is simple:
orders table: SELECT for all authenticated users. INSERT, UPDATE for all authenticated users.
customers table: SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE for all authenticated users.
notifications table: SELECT, UPDATE only for own user_id. INSERT from server-side API routes only.
order_timeline table: SELECT for all authenticated. INSERT from server-side only.
9.1 How It Works — Step by Step
User starts typing in the Mobile Number field on Create Order page
After 3 characters are entered AND a 300ms debounce delay passes, the search triggers
The API performs a PostgreSQL ILIKE query: WHERE mobile_number LIKE '{input}%'
Returns up to 5 matching customer records (id, full_name, mobile_number, email, address)
Results display in a dropdown below the input field
User clicks a result → all customer fields auto-populate → dropdown closes
If user ignores suggestions and continues typing a new number → no customer selected → a new customer record will be created on order save
useState for the mobile number input value
useDebounce hook (from usehooks-ts, free) to delay the API call by 300ms
useQuery (React Query) with the debounced value as the query key — auto-triggers when value changes
Popover component from shadcn/ui wraps the dropdown for accessible open/close behavior
CommandList component from shadcn/ui renders the search results with keyboard support
On selection: call setValue for all related form fields using React Hook Form's setValue method
The 'selectedCustomer' state is tracked separately — null means new customer will be created
9.3 New Customer Creation on Order Submit
When a user types a mobile number not found in the database and submits the Create Order form:
The API route /api/orders (POST) receives the order data including customer details
It checks if a customer with that mobile_number already exists
If not: INSERT into customers table first, then use the new customer_id for the order
If yes (race condition guard): use existing customer_id
Order is then created with the correct customer_id and a customer_snapshot JSON
Used everywhere to visually communicate order status. A single <StatusBadge status='pending' /> component handles all styling:
Dashboard stat cards. Props: title, value, icon, trend (positive/negative), trendPercent, onClick (optional):
White card with subtle shadow and rounded corners
Trend arrow below: ↑ +12% in green or ↓ -5% in red
Hover: slight shadow increase and cursor pointer if clickable
Vertical timeline rendered from an array of timeline events. Used on Order Detail page:
Vertical line running down the center-left
Each event: Colored circle dot on the line (color = status color), Event text ('Order marked as Out for Delivery'), User attribution ('by Rahul Sharma'), Timestamp
Most recent event at the top (newest first)
Smooth entrance animation for each entry
'Add Note' button at the bottom of the timeline
Used in both the Notification Panel (slide-over) and the /notifications page:
Left: Colored icon based on notification type (green package = new order, blue arrow = status update, red X = cancelled)
Center: Bold message text (bolder if unread), Smaller metadata: order ID link, time ago
Right: Blue dot indicator if unread
Click: Mark as read (remove blue dot), navigate to order
Shown when tables or lists have no results:
Subtitle: Contextual message based on whether filters are active
CTA button (optional): e.g., 'Create First Order', 'Clear Filters'
Used at the top of every page. Props: title, subtitle, breadcrumb, actions (JSX slot for buttons):
Title in large text, subtitle smaller below
Actions slot: Right-aligned area for CTA buttons
Sticky on scroll so navigation context is always visible
Step-by-step free hosting on Vercel + Supabase
Go to supabase.com → Create new project (free tier)
Wait for project to provision → go to Project Settings → API
Copy: Project URL, anon public key, service_role key (keep secret!)
Go to SQL Editor in Supabase dashboard
Run the CREATE TABLE statements from Section 8 of this document
Enable Realtime for tables: orders, notifications (in Supabase Dashboard → Database → Replication → Tables)
Configure RLS policies as described in Section 8.7
Push your code to GitHub (fork of MakerKit with your additions)
Go to vercel.com → New Project → Import your GitHub repo
Add Environment Variables in Vercel project settings:
Click Deploy — Vercel builds and deploys automatically
Every git push to main triggers automatic re-deployment
Preview deployments created for every pull request
500MB storage, 5GB bandwidth/month — more than enough for internal order tracking
200 concurrent connections free — sufficient for an internal team
50,000 monthly active users — unlimited for your team size
100GB bandwidth, serverless function executions — more than enough for internal tools
Only if you exceed ~5,000 orders/month AND have many concurrent users simultaneously
Clone repo, run npm install, configure .env.local with Supabase credentials
npm run dev — app runs on localhost:3000
Make changes → git commit → git push → Vercel auto-deploys in ~60 seconds
Use Supabase Studio (web UI) to inspect database, run queries, and manage users
Use Vercel logs and analytics to monitor performance and errors
What to build in what order
Build features in this sequence to have a working product as early as possible:
MakerKit setup, Supabase tables, auth flow, sidebar navigation, basic layout
Create order form (no autocomplete yet), order list, order detail page, status badges
Status update modal, timeline recording, cancel order, status transitions
Mobile number search API, debounced autocomplete dropdown, customer creation
KPI cards, order volume chart, status donut chart, recent orders table
Notifications table, bell icon, notification panel, /notifications page
Supabase Realtime subscriptions, toast notifications, live bell badge updates
Empty states, loading skeletons, mobile responsiveness, error handling, search/filter
~18–25 days to MVP with all features
Saves 5–7 days of auth and setup work
Complete blueprint documented. Ready to build.
Foundation: MakerKit · Next.js · Supabase · Tailwind · shadcn/ui · Vercel
